output: Harper Lee’s Go Set a Watchman, written before To Kill a Mockingbird and featuring many of the same characters, will be published when the 88-year-old author has been examined by the Alabama Securities Commission. The investigation, sparked by concerns about whether Lee could consent to the manuscript’s release, concluded with Lee answering questions to investigators’ satisfaction. Lee’s lawyer said she was “extremely hurt” by allegations she’d been manipulated. The probe was reportedly prompted by a doctor’s concerns and reported by the New York Times; To Kill a Mockingbird, published in 1960, has sold more than 40 million copies. The novel’s plot centers on an adult Scout Finch returning to Alabama from New York to visit her father.
